BPI Get Debug Info

BPI Get 3P Trace Params
Target is ALS Camera BPI
Step 2: Calculate Target Camera Rotation. Use the Control Rotation and interpolate for smooth camera rotation.
nodes.comment: Step 2: Calculate Target Camera Rotation. Use the Control Rotation and interpolate for smooth camera rotation.

nodes.title: Step 3: Calculate the Smoothed Pivot Target (Orange Sphere). Get the 3P Pivot Target (Green Sphere) and interpolate using axis independent lag for maximum control.
nodes.comment: Step 3: Calculate the Smoothed Pivot Target (Orange Sphere). Get the 3P Pivot Target (Green Sphere) and interpolate using axis independent lag for maximum control.

nodes.title: Step 4: Calculate Pivot Location (BlueSphere). Get the Smoothed Pivot Target and apply local offsets for further camera control.
nodes.comment: Step 4: Calculate Pivot Location (BlueSphere). Get the Smoothed Pivot Target and apply local offsets for further camera control.

nodes.title: Step 5: Calculate Target Camera Location. Get the Pivot location and apply camera relative offsets.
nodes.comment: Step 5: Calculate Target Camera Location. Get the Pivot location and apply camera relative offsets.

nodes.title: Step 6: Trace for an object between the camera and character to apply a corrective offset. Trace origins are set within the Character BP via the Camera Interface. Functions like the normal spring arm, but can allow for different trace origins regardless of the pivot.
nodes.comment: Step 6: Trace for an object between the camera and character to apply a corrective offset. Trace origins are set within the Character BP via the Camera Interface. Functions like the normal spring arm, but can allow for different trace origins regardless of the pivot.
